title: chelone glabra subsp. elatior
intro: Chelone glabra subsp. elatior, commonly known as the tall turtlehead, is a perennial plant species native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Chelone glabra subsp. elatior include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, and are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
